100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 16, 1926, the British Empire faced significant unrest as a general strike loomed, with labor unions rallying for better wages and working conditions. In the United States, tensions rose in the wake of ongoing debates over Prohibition, as lawmakers grappled with the implications of illegal speakeasies. Meanwhile, the political landscape in Europe remained volatile, with rising fascist sentiments and economic challenges troubling several nations.
